zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward de Chandos於大約1285年出生在英國德比夏爾拉德布恩時，他的父親John de Chandos27歲，母親Elizabeth Braylesford14歲。他和Elizabeth Twyford至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1340年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward de Chandos于大约1285年出生在英国德比夏爾拉德布恩时，他的父亲John de Chandos27岁，母亲Elizabeth Braylesford14岁。他和Elizabeth Twyford至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1340年之后。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chandos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Candos in Illeville-sur-Montfort (Eure).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward de Chandos was born about 1285, in Radbourne, Derbyshire, England, his father, John de Chandos, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth Braylesford, was 14. He had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Elizabeth Twyford. He died after 1340.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward de Chandos est né environ 1285, à Radbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Chandos, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Braylesford, avait 14 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Elizabeth Twyford. Il est décédé après 1340.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward de Chandos um 1285 in Radbourne, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Chandos 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Braylesford 14. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Twyford mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb nach 1340.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward de Chandos è nato il circa 1285 nel Radbourne, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, John de Chandos, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Braylesford, aveva 14 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Elizabeth Twyford. Morì dopo il 1340.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward de Chandosが約1285年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアラドボウルンで生まれた時，父John de Chandosは27歳，母Elizabeth Braylesfordは14歳であった。彼はElizabeth Twyfordとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1340年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward de Chandos(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 더비실르 라드보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Chandos(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Braylesford(은)는 14세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Twyford(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1340년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward de Chandos nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Radbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Chandos, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Braylesford, tinha 14 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas com Elizabeth Twyford. Ele faleceu após 1340.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward de Chandos родился около 1285 г. в местности радбурн, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Chandos было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Braylesford, 14. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Elizabeth Twyford. Он умер после 1340 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward de Chandos nació aproximadamente 1285, en Radbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Chandos, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Braylesford, tenía 14 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Elizabeth Twyford. Murió después de 1340.
